crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: pty
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
This crate provides:
- Terminal grid state (via alacritty_terminal)
- ANSI escape sequence parsing (via vte)
- PTY process management (via portable-pty)

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: SemanticZone
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Semantic zone type from OSC 133 shell integration

OSC 133 sequences mark boundaries between prompt, input, and output regions.
This allows the terminal to apply different rendering (e.g., glow on prompt/input only).

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: ShellEvent
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Shell events that can trigger theme effects

These events are detected from terminal output and can be used
to trigger visual effects defined in theme CSS (::on-bell, etc.)

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: TerminalEventProxy
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Terminal event handler that collects events for the application.

Uses a lock-free `SegQueue` instead of `Mutex<Vec>` to avoid contention
on the rendering hot path where `take_events()` is called every frame.

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: process_input
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Process input bytes through the terminal parser

Selection is preserved across output processing to support copy/paste
during active shell output (e.g., during builds, long-running commands).

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: scan_osc133
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Scan input bytes for OSC 133 semantic prompt sequences

OSC 133 format: `\x1b]133;X\x07` or `\x1b]133;X\x1b\\`
Where X is: A (prompt start), B (command start), C (output start), D (output end)
For D, may include exit code: `\x1b]133;D;exitcode\x07`

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: handle_osc133
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment: Handle an OSC 133 command

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: get_line_zone
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Get semantic zone for a given line

Returns Unknown if no OSC 133 marker has been seen for this line.

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: has_semantic_zones
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Check if any OSC 133 zones have been detected

Returns true if the shell has sent at least one OSC 133 sequence,
indicating it supports semantic prompts.

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Get damage information since last reset

Returns which parts of the terminal have changed and need redrawing.
Call `reset_damage()` after rendering to clear the damage state.

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: reset_damage
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Reset damage state after rendering

Call this after you've rendered the damaged regions to clear the
damage tracking for the next frame.

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: has_damage
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment: Check if any damage exists (needs redraw)

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: damaged_line_set
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Get the set of damaged line indices (0-based visible lines).

Returns `None` for full damage (caller should re-render everything).
Returns `Some(set)` with the indices of lines that changed for partial damage.

Check for title change and return it, preserving other events

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: check_events
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Check for terminal events and return title changes and bell triggers
Returns (Option<title>, bell_triggered)

Note: For theme-triggerable events, prefer `take_shell_events()` which
provides a unified `ShellEvent` enum including bell, command success/fail.

crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.name: take_shell_events
crates/crt-core/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Take shell events for theme triggers (bell, command success/fail)

This returns events that can trigger visual effects defined in theme CSS
(::on-bell, ::on-command-success, ::on-command-fail).

Also checks for Bell from alacritty_terminal and includes it as ShellEvent::Bell.
Returns (shell_events, Option<title>) to combine with title checking.

crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/grid.rs.name: Rgba
crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/grid.rs.doc_comment:
- `--grid-curved: true|false` (curved or straight vertical lines)
- `--grid-vanishing-spread: <number>` (0-1, how spread lines are at horizon)
- `--grid-glow-intensity: <number>` (0-1 glow brightness)
- `--grid-glow-radius: <number>` (glow spread in pixels, 0 = no glow)
- `--grid-animation-speed: <number>`
- `--grid-intensity: <number>` (0-1 opacity)
- `--grid-horizon: <number>` (0 = top, 1 = bottom)
- `--grid-perspective: <number>` (0 = flat, higher = more perspective)
- `--grid-line-width: <number>`
- `--grid-spacing: <number>` (grid density)
- `--grid-color: <color>`
- `--grid-enabled: true|false`

## CSS Properties

horizontal lines use perspective spacing, and the whole grid animates/scrolls.
Vertical lines converge toward a vanishing point with perspective curvature,
Renders a retro-style perspective grid below a configurable horizon line.

Grid backdrop effect - perspective grid with animation

- `--grid-vanishing-spread: <number>` (0-1, how spread lines are at horizon)
- `--grid-glow-intensity: <number>` (0-1 glow brightness)
- `--grid-glow-radius: <number>` (glow spread in pixels, 0 = no glow)
- `--grid-animation-speed: <number>`
- `--grid-intensity: <number>` (0-1 opacity)
- `--grid-horizon: <number>` (0 = top, 1 = bottom)
- `--grid-perspective: <number>` (0 = flat, higher = more perspective)
- `--grid-line-width: <number>`
- `--grid-spacing: <number>` (grid density)
- `--grid-color: <color>`
- `--grid-enabled: true|false`

## CSS Properties

horizontal lines use perspective spacing, and the whole grid animates/scrolls.
Vertical lines converge toward a vanishing point with perspective curvature,
Renders a retro-style perspective grid below a configurable horizon line.

Grid backdrop effect - perspective grid with animation

crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/mod.rs.signature: { fn effect_type(), fn update(), fn render(), fn configure(), fn is_enabled(), fn prepare_gpu_resources(), fn needs_gpu_...
crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Trait for backdrop effects that render to a Vello scene

Effects are updated each frame with delta time and total elapsed time,
then render their content to a shared Vello scene.

crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/mod.rs.name: prepare_gpu_resources
crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Prepare GPU resources for effects that need persistent textures.

Called before rendering when GPU resources are available.
Effects can use `renderer.register_texture()` to pre-upload textures
that bypass vello's atlas system, preventing memory growth.

Default implementation does nothing.

crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/mod.rs.name: needs_gpu_resources
crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Check if GPU resources need to be prepared or updated.

Returns true if `prepare_gpu_resources` should be called.
Default implementation returns false.

crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/mod.rs.name: cleanup_gpu_resources
crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Cleanup GPU resources when effect is disabled or removed.

Called to unregister textures and free GPU memory.
Default implementation does nothing.

crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/motion.r.name: MotionBehavior
crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/motion.r.doc_comment:
- `Orbit` - Circular path around center
- `Float` - Gentle random drift
- `Scroll` - Moves in direction, wraps around
- `Bounce` - DVD-logo style, bounces off edges
- `None` - Static position, no movement

## Available Behaviors

positional effects like ShapeEffect and SpriteEffect.

crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/motion.r.name: update
crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/motion.r.signature: (&mut self, position: &mut Vec2, size: Vec2, bounds: Rect, dt: f32, time: f32)
crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/motion.r.doc_comment:
Update position based on motion behavior

# Arguments
* `position` - Current position to update
* `size` - Size of the moving object (for collision)
* `bounds` - Screen/container bounds
* `dt` - Delta time in seconds
* `time` - Total elapsed time in seconds (for float behavior)

        target_view: &wgpu::TextureView,
        width: u32,
        height: u32,
    ) -> bool
crates/crt-renderer/src/effects/renderer.doc_comment:
Render all enabled effects directly to a target texture view

This renders the effects scene directly to the provided target view,
which can be the frame's surface texture for direct rendering.

Returns true if rendering occurred, false if skipped (no effects enabled
or invalid size).

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: DEFAULT_TOLERANCE
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment: Default tolerance: allow up to 0.5% of pixels to differ.

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: ComparisonResult
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.signature: { matched: bool, diff_percentage: f64, diff_pixels: usize, total_pixels: usize, diff_image: Option<Vec<u8>> }
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment: Result of comparing a rendered frame against a golden file.

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: PIXEL_THRESHOLD
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment:
Per-pixel color distance threshold.
Two pixels are "different" if the max channel delta exceeds this value.
This accounts for minor anti-aliasing and rounding differences.

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: compare_with_golden
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment:
Compare actual rendered pixels (raw RGBA) against a golden file.

`actual_png` is PNG-encoded bytes. `golden_path` should point to the
platform-specific golden file (use [`golden_path`] to resolve it).
`tolerance` is a percentage (0.0 – 100.0) of pixels allowed to differ.

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: compare_images
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment: Compare two RGBA images with the given tolerance percentage.

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: golden_path
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve the platform-specific golden file path for a test name.

Returns `{golden_dir}/{test_name}.{platform}.png` where platform
is `macos` or `linux`.

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: update_golden
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment: Write the actual image as the new golden file (for `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1`).

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: save_failure_artifacts
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment: Save failure artifacts (actual + diff) to the failures directory.

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: should_update_golden
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment: Check if golden files should be updated (via `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1`).

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: env_tolerance
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment: Read the tolerance override from `VISUAL_TEST_TOLERANCE`, if set.

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: assert_visual_match
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.signature:
(
    actual_png: &[u8],
    test_name: &str,
    project_root: &Path,
    tolerance: Option<f64>,
) -> ComparisonResult
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment:
Convenience function: run a full visual comparison for a test.

Handles UPDATE_GOLDEN, platform-aware path resolution, artifact saving,
and returns the comparison result with a descriptive error message.

crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.name: tests
crates/crt-renderer/src/golden.rs.doc_comment:
```
test_name.diff.png
test_name.actual.png
failures/         # Generated on failure (gitignored)
test_name.linux.png
test_name.macos.png
golden/           # Reference images (checked into git)
tests/visual/
```text

# Directory layout

- `VISUAL_TEST_TOLERANCE`: Override the default tolerance (0.5%) for all tests.
- `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1`: Write actual output as the new golden file instead of comparing.

# Environment variables

and generates diff images on failure.
configurable perceptual tolerance. Supports per-platform golden files
Compares rendered frames against reference ("golden") images with

Golden file comparison framework for visual regression testing.

test_name.diff.png
test_name.actual.png
failures/         # Generated on failure (gitignored)
test_name.linux.png
test_name.macos.png
golden/           # Reference images (checked into git)
tests/visual/
```text

# Directory layout

- `VISUAL_TEST_TOLERANCE`: Override the default tolerance (0.5%) for all tests.
- `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1`: Write actual output as the new golden file instead of comparing.

# Environment variables

and generates diff images on failure.
configurable perceptual tolerance. Supports per-platform golden files
Compares rendered frames against reference ("golden") images with

Golden file comparison framework for visual regression testing.

crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.name: GridRenderer
crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.signature: { shared: Arc<SharedGridPipeline>, globals_buffer: wgpu::Buffer, instance_capacity: usize, bind_group: Option<wgpu::Bind...
crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.doc_comment:
Grid renderer using instanced quads

The renderer does not own its instance buffer - this allows for buffer pooling
across window lifecycles. Use `create_instance_buffer()` to create a buffer,
or provide one from a buffer pool.

Pipeline objects (pipeline, bind group layout, sampler) can be shared across
windows via `new_with_shared()` to avoid duplicating Metal shader caches.

crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.name: GridRenderer
crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.doc_comment:
All text renders in a single draw call.
Each glyph is one instance with position, UV coords, and color.
Renders glyphs as instanced quads, sampling from a glyph atlas.

Grid renderer for terminal text using instanced quads

crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.name: MAX_INSTANCES
crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.doc_comment: Maximum number of glyph instances per render call

crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.name: INSTANCE_BUFFER_SIZE
crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.doc_comment: Size of instance buffer in bytes (32K instances * 48 bytes = 1.5 MB)

crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.name: create_instance_buffer
crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.doc_comment:
Create an instance buffer for use with this renderer

Call this to create a buffer if not using a buffer pool.
The buffer can be reused across renderer instances.

crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.name: new_with_shared
crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.doc_comment:
Create a grid renderer using shared pipeline objects.

This avoids duplicating the compiled Metal pipeline and shader caches
across multiple windows, saving ~5-15 MB per additional window.

crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.name: new
crates/crt-renderer/src/grid_renderer.rs.doc_comment:
Create a grid renderer with its own pipeline objects.

Prefer `new_with_shared()` when creating multiple renderers to avoid
duplicating GPU pipeline state.

The renderer does not own its instance buffer - this allows for buffer pooling
across window lifecycles. Use `create_instance_buffer()` to create a buffer,
or provide one from a buffer pool.

Pipeline objects can be shared across windows via `new_with_shared()` to
avoid duplicating Metal shader caches.

All rectangles render in a single draw call.
Renders colored rectangles for cell backgrounds.

Rect renderer for solid color rectangles using instanced quads

crates/crt-renderer/src/shared_pipelines.doc_comment:
All shared render pipelines, created once and shared across windows.

This reduces per-window GPU memory by avoiding duplicate Metal shader
compilation caches. With 3 windows and ~12 pipelines each, this saves
~24 duplicate pipeline compilations.

crates/crt-renderer/src/shared_pipelines.name: SharedPipelines
crates/crt-renderer/src/shared_pipelines.doc_comment:
separate — only the compiled pipeline objects are shared.
Per-window state (uniform buffers, bind groups, instance data) remains

use the same shaders.
compilation caches (~5-15 MB per pipeline on macOS) when multiple windows
and can be shared across all windows. This avoids duplicating Metal shader
Render pipelines, bind group layouts, and samplers are immutable after creation

Shared GPU pipeline objects for multi-window rendering.

crates/crt-renderer/src/sprite_renderer..doc_comment:
Render the sprite

# Arguments
* `pass` - Render pass to draw into
* `queue` - Queue for updating uniforms
* `frame` - Current animation frame
* `screen_width` - Screen width in pixels
* `screen_height` - Screen height in pixels
* `x` - Sprite center X position in pixels
* `y` - Sprite center Y position in pixels
* `scale` - Display scale factor
* `opacity` - Opacity (0.0-1.0)

crates/crt-renderer/src/sprite_renderer..name: apply_patch
crates/crt-renderer/src/sprite_renderer..doc_comment:
Apply a sprite patch, overriding specific properties

The patch preserves the current position and motion pattern while
allowing properties like fps, opacity, scale, and motion_speed to
be temporarily overridden.

Note: Changing the sprite path requires device/queue and is not
yet supported - patches can only modify animation properties.

crates/crt-renderer/src/tab_bar/mod.rs.name: layout
crates/crt-renderer/src/tab_bar/mod.rs.doc_comment:
GPU-accelerated tab bar with theme support, separated into:
- state: Tab data and management (no GPU)
- layout: Positioning and hit testing (no GPU)
- vello_renderer: Shape rendering via vello (backgrounds, borders)

Text and glow effects are rendered separately via the text pipeline,
triggered by CSS properties like `text-shadow`.

Move a tab from one index to another, updating active_tab to follow.

If the active tab is the one being moved, it tracks to the new position.
If the active tab is between `from` and `to`, it shifts by ±1.

crates/crt-renderer/src/tab_bar/vello_re.name: VelloTabBarRenderer
crates/crt-renderer/src/tab_bar/vello_re.signature: { scene: Scene, target_texture: Option<wgpu::Texture>, target_view: Option<wgpu::TextureView>, target_size: (u32, u32) }
crates/crt-renderer/src/tab_bar/vello_re.doc_comment:
Vello-based tab bar renderer

Uses a shared vello::Renderer passed in during render calls to save memory.
Each instance maintains its own Scene and render target.

Pattern:
1. Build shapes into Scene during prepare()
2. Render Scene to texture via shared vello renderer
3. Text/glow effects rendered separately by existing pipeline

crates/crt-renderer/src/tab_bar/vello_re.name: VelloTabBarRenderer
crates/crt-renderer/src/tab_bar/vello_re.doc_comment:
The expensive vello::Renderer is shared across all renderers to save memory.
Text glow effects are still handled by the existing text renderer.
Renders tab bar shapes (backgrounds, borders, rounded rects) using vello.

Vello-based tab bar rendering

crates/crt-theme/src/parser.rs.name: apply_ansi_palette
crates/crt-theme/src/parser.rs.signature:
(
    theme: &mut Theme,
    custom: &HashMap<String, String>,
) -> Result<(), ThemeParseError>
crates/crt-theme/src/parser.rs.doc_comment:
Apply ANSI palette colors from custom properties
Supports multiple naming conventions:
- --ansi-black, --ansi-red, etc. (preferred)
- --ansi-bright-black, --ansi-bright-red, etc. (preferred)
- --color-black, --color-red, etc. (legacy)
- --color-bright-black, etc. (legacy)

src/bin/profile_gpu_memory.rs.name: main
src/bin/profile_gpu_memory.rs.doc_comment:
```
cargo run --release --bin profile_gpu_memory
```sh

# Usage

buffers, glyph atlas, staging buffers, and pipeline state.
GPU allocations that are invisible to system allocators: textures, vertex
Unlike `profile_memory` (DHAT-based, CPU heap only), this tool tracks

reports GPU-side resource usage (glyph atlas, instance buffers, textures).
Exercises the full rendering pipeline via headless GPU rendering and

GPU memory profiling tool.

src/bin/profile_gpu_memory.rs.name: load_system_font
src/bin/profile_gpu_memory.rs.doc_comment: Load a monospace font from system fonts.

src/bin/profile_memory.rs.name: ALLOC
src/bin/profile_memory.rs.doc_comment:
Exercises the terminal hot paths and produces a DHAT allocation profile.

# Usage

```sh
cargo run --release --features dhat-heap --bin profile_memory
```

This produces `dhat-heap.json` in the current directory.
Open it with the DHAT viewer: https://nnethercote.github.io/dh_view/dh_view.html

src/bin/profile_memory.rs.name: main
src/bin/profile_memory.rs.doc_comment:
Simulate realistic terminal workload:
- Scrolling output (log-like)
- Rapid in-place updates (htop-like)
- Color-heavy output
- Unicode / wide characters

src/gpu/buffer_pool.rs.name: new
src/gpu/buffer_pool.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new buffer pool

# Arguments
* `device` - The wgpu device for creating buffers
* `max_per_class` - Maximum buffers to keep pooled per class

src/gpu/buffer_pool.rs.name: checkout
src/gpu/buffer_pool.rs.doc_comment:
Check out a buffer from the pool

Returns a pooled buffer if available, otherwise allocates a new one.
The buffer is automatically returned to the pool when the PooledBuffer is dropped.
Returns None if the pool lock is poisoned.

Call this after closing windows to free up GPU memory.

src/gpu/mod.rs.name: ensure_shared_pipelines
src/gpu/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Ensure shared render pipelines are initialized for the given surface format.

Called on first window creation when the surface format is known.
Subsequent calls are no-ops.

src/gpu/mod.rs.name: ensure_vello_renderer
src/gpu/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Ensure the Vello renderer is initialized (lazy initialization)

Call this when you need advanced CSS effects like rounded corners,
gradients, backdrop effects, or complex paths. The renderer is
cached after first creation.

src/gpu/mod.rs.name: vello_renderer_arc
src/gpu/mod.rs.doc_comment: Get a clone of the shared Vello renderer Arc for passing to EffectsRenderer

src/gpu/mod.rs.name: reset_vello_renderer
src/gpu/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Reset the Vello renderer to clean up accumulated texture atlas resources.

Vello's internal atlas/texture caches grow over time and don't have a
built-in cleanup mechanism. Recreating the renderer periodically prevents
unbounded GPU memory growth.

src/gpu/mod.rs.name: cleanup
src/gpu/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Explicitly release GPU resources before dropping.

Call this before removing WindowState to ensure proper cleanup.
This unconfigures the surface which releases swap chain buffers (IOSurface on macOS).
Note: PooledTextures (text_texture, crt_texture) are automatically returned
to the pool when dropped, so we don't explicitly destroy them here.

Returns None if the pool lock is poisoned.

src/input/drag.rs.name: compute_reorder_index
src/input/drag.rs.signature:
(
    cursor_x: f32,
    tab_rects: &[crt_renderer::TabRect],
    dragged_index: usize,
) -> usize
src/input/drag.rs.doc_comment:
Compute the insertion index for tab reordering based on cursor position.

Given the cursor's x-position within the tab bar, the tab rects, and the
index of the tab being dragged, returns the target index where the tab
should be moved to.

The logic: for each tab rect, if the cursor is past the midpoint, the
insertion point is after that tab. The dragged tab is excluded from
the comparison since it's "in flight".

src/input/drag.rs.name: resolve_drop_target
src/input/drag.rs.signature:
(
    cursor_screen: PhysicalPosition<f64>,
    source_window_id: WindowId,
    dragged_index: usize,
    source_tab_count: usize,
    windows: &[WindowScreenRect],
) -> DragDropTarget
src/input/drag.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve what drop target the cursor is over.

Pure function: given the cursor's screen-space position, the source window ID,
source tab count, and all window rects, returns the appropriate `DragDropTarget`.

Resolution priority:
1. Over a non-source window's tab bar → `Merge`
2. Over the source window's tab bar → `Reorder` (only if source has >1 tab)
3. Over any window's body (not tab bar) → `Detach` (only if source has >1 tab)
4. Outside all windows → `Detach` (only if source has >1 tab)

When `source_tab_count == 1`, only `Merge` is valid — reorder is meaningless
and detach would just recreate the same window. Returns `Pending` for invalid targets.

src/input/drag.rs.name: should_start_drag
src/input/drag.rs.signature:
(
    tab_bar: &crt_renderer::TabBar,
    context_menu_visible: bool,
    x: f32,
    y: f32,
) -> Option<TabId>
src/input/drag.rs.doc_comment:
Check whether a tab drag should be initiated for a mouse press at (x, y).

Returns `Some(tab_id)` if:
- The click hits a tab (not the close button)
- The window has more than 1 tab (last-tab guard)
- The tab bar is not in edit mode
- The context menu is not visible

Returns `None` if the click should be handled normally.

src/input/mod.rs.name: mouse_report
src/input/mod.rs.signature: (button: u8, col: usize, line: usize, pressed: bool, sgr_mode: bool) -> Vec<u8>
src/input/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Generate mouse escape sequence for terminal

# Arguments
* `button` - Mouse button code (0=left, 1=middle, 2=right, 3=release, 32+=motion, 64/65=scroll)
* `col` - Column (0-indexed)
* `line` - Line (0-indexed)
* `pressed` - Whether this is a press event (for SGR mode)
* `sgr_mode` - Whether to use SGR extended encoding

src/input/mod.rs.name: get_clipboard_content
src/input/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Get clipboard content from system clipboard

Handles three types of clipboard content:
1. Text - returns the text directly
2. Files (copied from Finder/Explorer) - returns the file path(s)
3. Images (screenshots) - saves to temp file and returns the path

This allows pasting images into applications like Claude Code that accept file paths.

Paste content to terminal with bracketed paste mode support

If the terminal has bracketed paste mode enabled, the content will be
wrapped with escape sequences to indicate a paste operation.

src/input/mouse.rs.name: GridLayout
src/input/mouse.rs.signature: { content_offset_x: f32, content_offset_y: f32, padding: f32, cell_width: f32, line_height: f32, max_cols: usize, max_ro...
src/input/mouse.rs.doc_comment: Layout parameters needed for coordinate conversion

src/input/mouse.rs.name: screen_to_grid_position
src/input/mouse.rs.signature: (x: f32, y: f32, layout: &GridLayout) -> Option<(usize, usize)>
src/input/mouse.rs.doc_comment:
Convert screen pixel coordinates to terminal grid position (col, line).

Returns `None` if the position is outside the terminal content area
(negative relative coordinates after subtracting offsets and padding).
Clamps to grid bounds when inside the content area.

src/input/mouse.rs.name: MouseClickTarget
src/input/mouse.rs.signature: OpenUrl | ContextSubmenuItem | ContextMenuItem | ContextSubmenuParent | DismissContextMenu | MoveContextMenu | ShowConte...
src/input/mouse.rs.doc_comment: What a mouse click targets

src/input/mouse.rs.name: determine_click_target
src/input/mouse.rs.signature:
(
    button: MouseButton,
    button_state: ElementState,
    cmd_pressed: bool,
    context_menu_visible: bool,
    has_grid_position: bool,
) -> MouseClickTarget
src/input/mouse.rs.doc_comment:
Determine what a mouse click targets, without performing any side effects.

This is the pure decision function for `handle_mouse_input`. The caller
is responsible for executing the appropriate side effects based on the result.

src/input/mouse.rs.name: compute_click_count
src/input/mouse.rs.signature:
(
    now: Instant,
    last_click_time: Option<Instant>,
    last_click_pos: Option<(usize, usize)>,
    current_pos: (usize, usize),
    previous_count: usize,
    threshold: Duration,
    max_distance: usize,
) -> usize
src/input/mouse.rs.doc_comment:
Compute click count for multi-click detection (single, double, triple).

Returns 1 for single click, 2 for double, 3 for triple. Cycles back to 1
after triple click. Resets to 1 if the click is too far from the previous
click or too much time has elapsed.

src/input/mouse.rs.name: normalize_scroll_delta
src/input/mouse.rs.doc_comment:
Normalize a pixel scroll delta to a line count.

For `LineDelta`, returns the Y component directly.
For `PixelDelta`, divides by line height to convert pixels to lines.

src/input/mouse.rs.name: handle_cursor_moved
src/input/mouse.rs.doc_comment:
Handle cursor moved event

Updates cursor position, context menu hover, URL hover, and selection drag.

src/input/mouse.rs.signature:
(

    button: MouseButton,
    button_state: ElementState,
    modifiers: &Modifiers,
) -> bool
src/input/mouse.rs.doc_comment:
Handle mouse button event

Returns true if the event was fully handled.
